title: cracca tenuifolia
intro: Cracca tenuifolia, also known as slender bird's foot trefoil, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the Cracca tenuifolia plant are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
